title: Rename and relocate
prompt: mv does double duty: it MOVES files and RENAMES them — a rename is just a move to a new name. Your lab has draft.txt and a folder final/. End state: the file lives at final/report.txt, renamed AND moved, with nothing left behind in the lab root. One command can do both at once.

details.restate: Move draft.txt into the final folder AND rename it to report.txt in the process. Afterwards the root has no draft.txt and no report.txt — the one file lives at final/report.txt with its contents intact.

io.because: a rename is a move to a new name in the same place
the destination can be a new path and a new name at once

help.plain: One mv command can change a file's folder and its name at the same time.
help.analogy: Moving house and changing your nameplate in a single trip — the destination address includes the new name.
help.mnemonic: mv source destination — and the destination can be folder/newname.
help.nudge: What happens if the second thing you give mv is a path that ends in a different filename?
